deterministic_inputs_hash_coverage.claim: every recorded interaction in the seal carries a deterministic_inputs_hash (SHA-256 of the deterministic inputs that produced it) -> the run is replay-verifiable
deterministic_inputs_hash_coverage.sql: SELECT count(*) AS total, count(*) FILTER (WHERE raw_governance_snapshot ? 'deterministic_inputs_hash') AS with_hash, count(*) FILTER (WHERE raw_governance_snapshot IS NOT NULL) AS snapshot_nonnull FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-b6e4-4abe-94e6-a2e6ccfbefc1'

canonical_equals_kalman_exactness.claim: the shipped canonical mastery is exactly the single Kalman estimate (m = m_K) -- zero mismatch -> deterministic, replayable canonical
canonical_equals_kalman_exactness.sql: SELECT count(*) AS total, count(*) FILTER (WHERE canonical_mastery_after = kalman_mastery_after) AS exact_match, count(*) FILTER (WHERE canonical_mastery_after IS DISTINCT FROM kalman_mastery_after) AS mismatch, max(abs(canonical_mastery_after - kalman_mastery_after)) AS max_abs_diff FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-b6e4-4abe-94e6-a2e6ccfbefc1'
canonical_equals_kalman_exactness.ensemble_weights_check: ensemble_weight_kalman = 1.0 and ensemble_weight_bayesian = ensemble_weight_lyapunov = 0.0 on the inspected chain -- consistent with canonical = single Kalman (ensemble retained only for sigma^2).

mastery_after_is_NOT_canonical_on_this_s.important: The mastery_after COLUMN is NOT the canonical mastery on this seal. It is the inherited V1 3-learner ensemble synthesis. corr(mastery_after, kalman_mastery_after) = 0.7930, mean_abs_diff = 0.16374, max_abs_diff = 0.41415 (96,727 rows). Determinism of the SHIPPED canonical is proven via canonical_mastery_after (==kalman, 0 mismatch above), NOT via mastery_after.
mastery_after_is_NOT_canonical_on_this_s.sql: SELECT corr(mastery_after, kalman_mastery_after), avg(abs(mastery_after - kalman_mastery_after)), max(abs(mastery_after - kalman_mastery_after)) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-...' AND mastery_after IS NOT NULL AND kalman_mastery_after IS NOT NULL

seal_ledger_provenance.sql: SELECT seal_id, experiment_run_id, as_of_row_count, content_hash, sealed_at, frozen_stats->'code_provenance' FROM sealed_runs WHERE seal_id='seal-bae44d1a-a314-48f5-b145-c92e6cbe08d7'

code_provenance.note: code/pipeline version that generated this run; compare git_sha to HEAD to detect a stale-vs-code seal

seal_ledger_provenance.consistency: content_hash, as_of_row_count, and git_sha in the ledger all match the brief's stated seal -> the sealed artifact is the one being analyzed; git_dirty=false confirms a clean-tree build.

drift_F4_F9.note: This drift trace is from an EARLIER primary/replay pair (primary run-f985e8bf, replay run-6836b17a, audited 2026-05-22), NOT from this seal run-d2154070. It characterizes the L1->L5 signal-chain drift envelope used in section 4.10.1.1; it is the existing determinism evidence the brief points to.
drift_F4_F9.chain: per-learner JT contributions -> ensemble weights -> canonical/ensemble mastery -> mastery_after -> policy_score
drift_F4_F9.mechanical_verdict: policy_score max_abs_drift / mastery_after max_abs_drift = 4.435e-03 / 2.496e-03 = 1.78x -- within the runtime_bounded envelope (4e-2). policy_score is a deterministic function of mastery_after; its envelope inherits runtime_bounded.
drift_F4_F9.interpretation: On that primary/replay pair the floating-point drift across the full decision chain stays within ~4e-3 (decision_eps) and the policy-score envelope is ~1.78x the mastery envelope -- bounded, not divergent. This is the F-beta drift bound; it bounds non-bit-exactness of the live runtime synthesis (mastery_after / 3-learner). The SHIPPED canonical of THIS seal (canonical_mastery_after = m_K, single Kalman) is bit-exact-reproducible from its deterministic_inputs_hash (100% coverage) and equals kalman_mastery_after with 0 mismatch.

framing_note_governance_inheritance: The JT/ensemble-weight drift layers (L1, L2) above are 3-learner-runtime-driven; on this seal the canonical mastery is m_K but JT/ADC were inherited from the V1 runtime. The determinism guarantee that matters for the seal is (a) 100% deterministic_inputs_hash coverage and (b) canonical == m_K with 0 mismatch -- both demonstrated above from run-d2154070 directly. See research_validation/KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md.
